paragraphes: HYGIFRANCE specialises in building maintenance: the ones we live in, the ones we work in, the ones we manage. Our belief is simple: a clean building inspires trust, ages better and costs less to maintain over time.

We tailor every intervention to the place and its use. A co-ownership lobby isn't treated like a doctor's surgery; a grimy façade calls for a different approach than a stained sofa. Our teams arrive fully equipped, protect the surroundings and leave every space ready to use.
Based in Lille, we cover the whole of the Nord. A single point of contact, a clear quote and the same high standards, whether the job lasts an hour or forms part of a year-round maintenance contract.

image.alt: HYGIFRANCE agent maintaining a building lobby

items.texte: By phone, WhatsApp or through the online assistant, you tell us about the building and the job. A few photos help frame everything.
We assess the surface, condition and access, then give you a detailed quote with no commitment. You know exactly what's included.
A date is set to suit your constraints. The team arrives equipped, protects the premises and works with the right equipment for the surface.
Before leaving, we check every area. For regular maintenance, we agree together on a schedule that fits.

items.reponse: Of course. A one-time post-construction clean, a restoration before a check-out inspection or a sofa to freshen up: every request is quoted separately, with no contract required.

items.question: Is the quote free?
items.reponse: Yes, assessing your building and the quote are free and without commitment. We spell out what's included so you can decide with complete clarity, no surprises on the invoice.

items.reponse: Yes. In the event of water damage, fire or flooding, we organise quickly and can deal directly with your insurer for the restoration.

meta.title: Diogenes syndrome cleaning in Lille
meta.description: Restoration of severely cluttered or unsanitary homes in Lille and across the Nord: clearance, deep cleaning and disinfection, with discretion and respect.

content.titre: Diogenes syndrome: getting back on your feet, gently
content.intro: An overwhelmed home is never just a matter of housekeeping. We work with method, discretion and respect, without judgement.

paragraphes: Diogenes syndrome leads to an accumulation of objects and waste that makes a home barely liveable. Behind every job there is a person and a story: our first priority is respect. We move at the pace of the occupants and the family, in the utmost discretion, with neutral vehicles and clothing if you wish. For these situations, we work well beyond the Nord: we travel across all of France and all of Belgium.

In practice, we proceed in stages: sorting and clearing bulky items, disposal through the appropriate channels, deep cleaning of every surface, then disinfection and odour removal. Floors, walls, the kitchen and the bathrooms are restored until the interior is healthy and safe again.

Every situation is unique: the floor area, the level of clutter and the sanitary condition determine the duration and the price, always set by quote. We can coordinate the work with a relative, a guardian, a social landlord or social services.

Floors, walls, kitchen and bathrooms cleaned thoroughly to make the home genuinely clean again.
Treatment of contact surfaces and elimination of odours for a healthy, safe interior.
Neutral vehicles and clothing available, work organised in complete confidentiality.

We've chosen to cover every need a building can have — from routine upkeep of communal areas to the most delicate jobs — rather than sending you from one provider to the next.

Outdoor cleaning, communal areas, offices and shops, upholstery, Diogenes syndrome and disaster restoration: this breadth demands that we're meticulous with every action. We train our teams to adapt the method to the surface and to leave every place healthier than we found it.
Behind all of this, one simple standard: a clear quote we stick to, a single point of contact, and a result checked with you before we leave. That's what we want to be judged on.
